title: Radhapuram and the Constitution’s broken clock
description: A remedy that survives only after the right it was meant to protect has disappeared is hardly a remedy at all. Election law makes this problem especially acute because political office is held for a fixed and irretrievable term. A court may correct the record years later, but it cannot return to a constituency the representation it was denied.Article 329(b) of the Constitution nevertheless closes every door to an electoral challenge but one. An election may be questioned only through an election
